question: What is the term for the operational risks an institution is exposed to through its products, activities, processes and systems before any control or mitigation measures are taken into account?
Which plan sets out the procedures, recovery options and alternative resources needed to keep critical processes running and to recover them?
Following the catastrophic destruction of a bank's entire ICT infrastructure, which plan defines the recovery processes for achieving the recovery goals?
A significant disruption at a bank can be overcome with the predefined BCP and DRP — does it count as a crisis situation?
Does a tolerance for disruption have to be defined individually for every critical function?
Besides lowering the residual risks of disruptions, does operational resilience also reduce the inherent risk of disruptions occurring?
How often must the board of directors approve the institution's risk tolerance for operational risk?
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: Instead of the event types used for calculating the minimum capital requirements, may a bank categorise its operational risks using an internal taxonomy?
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: From which two risk perspectives must identified operational risks be assessed?
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: Who must regularly perform and document the assessment of the design and operating effectiveness of key controls?
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: A bank is about to make major changes to its payment processing system — must it carry out an ad hoc risk and control assessment before the change?
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: Depending on the type, scope, complexity and risk of an institution's products, activities, processes and systems, which tools and methods shall be applied in managing operational risk?
gold: collection and analysis of internal loss data
accept: systematic collection and analysis of loss data
internal loss data and relevant external events
risk and control indicators for monitoring
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: What information must the at least annual report to the executive board on the relevant ICT and cyber risks contain?
accept: development of ICT and cyber risks
effectiveness of the corresponding key controls
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: Must a systemically important bank also conduct regular operational risk reporting at the level of the business or organisational areas that are exposed to material operational risks?
section: IV. Operational risk management / A. Overarching operational risk management

question: The executive board must ensure that documented procedures, processes and controls with adequate qualified staffing are in place for which two ICT areas?
section: B. ICT risk management / a) ICT strategy and governance

question: Which three requirements shall change management focus on in particular during the development or procurement of ICT?
When procuring a new trading application, may a bank confine the testing and validation to the functional requirements only?
Is it enough for a bank to implement ICT back-up and recovery processes without testing and validating them on a regular basis?
An application has passed its planned decommissioning date but remains in use — may the institution simply keep operating it without any specific risk-oriented measures?
Which aspects must an institution's cyber risk management cover at a minimum, in accordance with internationally recognised standards and practices?
gold: protection of ICT assets and critical data
accept: identification of the institution-specific threat landscape
protective measures for ICT assets and electronic critical data
timely logging and detection of cyber attacks
prompt recovery after a cyber attack

question: For its regular vulnerability assessments and penetration tests, may a bank consider only those inventoried ICT assets that are reachable from the internet?
What must risk-based, threat intelligence-related scenario cyber exercises be conducted on the basis of?
Which governing body appoints the unit that establishes and monitors the framework for ensuring the confidentiality, integrity and availability of critical data?
May an institution identify its critical data only on an ad hoc basis, for example whenever new systems are introduced?
Over which period must the critical data defined by an institution be managed?
To speed up support requests, a bank grants all IT employees standing access to critical data — is this in line with supervisory requirements?
In the context of critical data, which group of people must be recorded in a list that is kept and updated on a regular basis?
How quickly must an institution report incidents that substantially impair the confidentiality, integrity or availability of critical data to FINMA?
May the criteria for assessing how a cloud provider handles critical data be checked for the first time after the contract has been concluded?
A bank defines RTO and RPO for its critical processes internally but does not coordinate them with the service providers involved — is this sufficient?
How many business continuity plans must an institution define at a minimum?
At what minimum frequency must the business impact analysis and the BCP be reviewed and updated?
Where critical processes are outsourced, must the disaster recovery plan take the external dependencies and contractual provisions into account?
Can tabletop exercises be used as one means of testing the implementation of the BCP and DRP?
How often, at a minimum, must the most important measures under the BCP and DRP and the crisis organisation be tested?
What type of scenarios must the tests of the BCP, DRP and crisis organisation encompass?
Is a single training session when joining the institution sufficient for members of the crisis organisation with regard to their BCM tasks?
When institutions provide financial services abroad, the risks arising from the application of what must be identified, limited and controlled?
section: F. Management of risks from cross-border service business

question: May a bank rely on a single generic service model for every foreign market it serves?
section: F. Management of risks from cross-border service business

question: A foreign-based subsidiary of a Swiss institution serves that institution's clients across borders — do the principles for managing cross-border risks apply to this situation as well?
section: F. Management of risks from cross-border service business

question: Which components of the risk management framework must be coordinated with one another so that they contribute to strengthening operational resilience?
accept: operational risk management including ICT and cyber risk management

question: What must be documented for the critical functions as a minimum?
May the regular tests of the ability to provide critical functions be confined to scenarios involving short and limited interruptions?
What are the services called that a systemically important bank needs in order to continue its systemically important functions during resolution, recovery or restructuring?
section: VI. Continuation of critical services during resolution and recovery of systemically important banks

question: Until the revised Capital Adequacy Ordinance enters into force, which FINMA circular remains authoritative for the capital requirements for operational risk?
section: B. Concerning the capital requirements for operational risk
